title: Aesthetics of Eva
wikitext:
[]: Aesthetics of Eva             Quote Post by Xacebans » Mon Jan 16, 2012 12:24 pm Is there anything about the art style you really like or dislike? What's your favorite character design? Your favorite Eva design? Any character designs that just rub you the wrong way? Personally, I like the art style as a whole but it irritates me when I see Rei and Asuka with unrealistically long legs for their heights, and sometimes these really elongated torsos. They'd be around 5'10 if they actually had proportions like they have in a lot of official art! For example, SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Mon Jan 16, 2012 12:34 pm Sadamoto's character designs are wildly impossible in proportion at any rate. I sometimes wonder if the Eva designs aren't a bit of snide commentary on that by Yamashita. The broad shoulders, large heads, wasp-waistedness, narrow hips... But no, there's nothing I really dislike except for Unit 00's perpetual O( face.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Jan 16, 2012 1:07 pm I don't understand why the plug suits aren't more uniform in design. Of course they have to be somewhat tailored to the individual user, but there's no reason for them to have wildly different little valves, hard chunks of plastic and random doohickies slapped on here and there. Whichever design works best would be sensible to adapt to all pilots rather than giving each something unique. It seems pointlessly wasteful that they even produced multiple designs in the first place rather than testing some alternatives before sticking to just one.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Jan 16, 2012 1:44 pm I loved the aesthetics by the time Ep 8 came around. It looked like all the character animation was finally in-house, rather than out-sourced and ammusingly off-model. Ep 1-6 seemed to have very little in-house character animation, and, as mentoned before, it looked rediculous by Yamashita.

[]:             Quote Post by BulletShoot » Mon Jan 16, 2012 1:48 pm I quite like the designs for Eva, it doesn't have the over colorfulness that a lot of anime tend to have nowadays. Also on the proportions of characters, they're pretty bad but I have seen much worse. I'm looking at you CLAMP.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Jan 16, 2012 3:30 pm Especially toward the latter half of the series, I really enjoy the animation for the faces of each character. It really manages to grasp the emotions that were nonexistent in some other episodes. One thing that bothers me nowadays is the lack of more interesting camera angles, though it can't really be helped. If I want pretty animation, I can go to Rebuild.

[]:             Quote Post by LeoXiao » Mon Jan 16, 2012 6:10 pm The animation of faces could be more uniform; in this regard I liked EoE's style quite a bit. In terms of general art, I thought NGE had good style. I liked especially the use of contrasting light and darkness, as well as captions, which did a lot toward embodying the setting of the underground NERV base and Instrumentality/mindrape scenes, respectively. One example I though was cool was when a SEELE guy would speak, and then a light would turn on and then we'd see the person in question (liek Gendo, Ritsuko, or Misato)., and that this technique was reused in the final episodes.

[]:             Quote Post by Fireball » Mon Jan 16, 2012 6:32 pm Sadamoto style was one of the first things that attracted me to the series. It combined the old school with modernism and has that perfect balance of style and realism. In that regard, he and Kenichi Sonoda are one of my favorite artists. I like the character designs very much. It's so 90s with the hard edges and scrawny limbs but still managed to preserve itself to this day. Can you say timeless? Just look at Rei for example. She radiates grace and inviolability when she stands infront of the moon. It is a picture for eternity. A tastement to conceptional branding. And the mecha design is so avantgarde and animalistic. It really challenged the status quo of what robots looked before. The Angels all feel so out of this world but still have this organic structure despite looking so extraterrestrial. Was never too fond of Jet Alone and the VTOL units I guess, and those exaggerated big rockets (however they look awesome when they explode) but overall everything is in touch with the setting.

[]:             Quote Post by Cruel Thesis » Mon Jan 16, 2012 11:20 pm Sometimes the waists of the female characters are drawn too thinly for my taste. I also don’t like it when the noses are too sharply pointed. I don’t think the characters are overly idealised. Though Rei and Asuka push it a bit. Im glad that the hair colours aren’t too crazy. Again Rei pushes that but she’s meant to stand apart from the others. Overall I think i liked EOE's style the most. Today a lot of shows seem to want to out-cute each other and produce stuff that seems really gaudy to me. I liked that eva presented some fantastic things from a grounded perspective. It didnt try too hard to dazzle.. I wont comment on the new movies though.

[]:             Quote Post by ReiAyanami25 » Tue Jan 17, 2012 12:25 am I like the way that the different eva units all look diffrent in some way, such as the colouration or the head design. That excludes the mass produced Evangelions. I also like the way the characters expressions are drawn, and the fact that the faces can display so much or so little emotion. Also, the way that Rei's smile was drawn, especially the version featured in Death, made that particular smile she made seem very special, as if she had never smiled that way in her whole life. It was one of the rare moments where Rei looked completely happy, the smile seemed so sincere. Some of the faces Shinjii made seemed very expressive too, almost providing us with a small window to glimpse his thoughts and inner feelings with, such as this shot here: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Xana » Tue Jan 17, 2012 12:44 am ReiAyanami25 wrote: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Cruel Thesis » Tue Jan 17, 2012 1:04 am Sometimes the characters nostrils disappear. But i think maybe thats a stylistic choice. Xana wrote: Is it weird that I find pointy noses sexy?? some people here seem to dislike the pics where characters are drawn with pointy noses, but I find them hot.

[]:             Quote Post by ReiAyanami25 » Tue Jan 17, 2012 1:15 am Xana wrote: Rei's character design has always been my favorite

[]:             Quote Post by Xana » Tue Jan 17, 2012 1:27 am Cruel Thesis wrote: Sometimes the characters nostrils disappear. But i think maybe thats a stylistic choice.

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Tue Jan 17, 2012 6:10 am I really like the character design in general. It's not too flashy, but not boring, and it's straight to the point. Also the animators manage to say a lot with facial expressions, that's great. As for the Evas, I am really fascinated, how they managed to make them so formidable although they have such flashy colours. What I like most, is the cutting. Anno adds a lot of power to the scenes just due to the cutting and, sometimes, the camera angels. But the most interesting thing is, that the show does not feel really outdated (yet?). I think the designs might become timeless. Overall, I like the aesthetics very much, IMO they fit EVA perfectly.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Tue Jan 17, 2012 5:50 pm Xana wrote: I wish real people didn't have nostrils, or cavities of any sort.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Jan 17, 2012 6:05 pm ^by budding. Now, back to the topic, please.

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Tue Jan 17, 2012 6:15 pm ReiAyanami25 wrote: One of the parts of the drawings that I did not like was this shot from the OP: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Lorkhan » Tue Jan 17, 2012 8:02 pm NemZ wrote: I don't understand why the plug suits aren't more uniform in design. Of course they have to be somewhat tailored to the individual user, but there's no reason for them to have wildly different little valves, hard chunks of plastic and random doohickies slapped on here and there. Whichever design works best would be sensible to adapt to all pilots rather than giving each something unique. It seems pointlessly wasteful that they even produced multiple designs in the first place rather than testing some alternatives before sticking to just one.

[]:             Quote Post by Final Messenger » Tue Jan 17, 2012 8:37 pm I like the visual style of Evangelion all around if I were to nitpick about one part though I guess it would be that I wish the body designs of the evas were diffrent but that is a minor complaint.
